ETLDelphi 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • delphi100k: Source Data and OMOP CDM Mapping
    • Delphi to OMOP CDM ETL: Workflow and Design Decisions
    • How to Run the ETL
    • run_etl: Detailed Steps and Mechanics

    Delphi to OMOP CDM ETL: Workflow and Design Decisions

    ETLDelphi

    2026-02-10

    etl.Rmd

    Introduction

    This vignette documents the Delphi/Delfi to OMOP CDM v5.4 ETL implemented in ETLDelphi: the end-to-end workflow, every major design decision, and how to run and extend it.

    The ETL transforms source tables (enrollment, encounter, provider, death, problem, medication_orders, medication_fulfillment, current_medications, immunization, lab_orders, lab_results, vital_sign, allergy, therapy_orders, therapy_actions) into OMOP Common Data Model v5.4 tables in DuckDB, using a 3-layer pattern (raw → staging → CDM), deterministic integer IDs, and explicit concept mapping with observable failures.

    Architecture Overview

    Three-Layer Pattern

    Layer Schema Purpose
    Source src Raw tables; all columns varchar as in the Delphi DDL. Pre-loaded by the user.
    Staging stg Typed and cleaned: parsed dates/numerics, trimmed strings, normalized codes. Reject tables capture parse failures.
    CDM cdm OMOP CDM v5.4 tables; integer IDs only; concept IDs from vocabulary or 0 when unmapped.

    All transformations are expressed as SQL scripts run in a fixed order. The R package only orchestrates: it runs those scripts in sequence via DBI + duckdb, substitutes schema names from config, and logs steps (and optionally run metadata) to stg.etl_run_log and stg.etl_step_log.

    Design Principles

    1. Integer IDs only in CDM — All OMOP tables reference integer primary/foreign keys. Source IDs (e.g. Member_ID, Encounter_ID) are varchar; the ETL builds deterministic key maps (stg.map_person, stg.map_visit, etc.) that assign stable integer IDs.
    2. Unmapped concepts = 0 — Per OMOP convention, missing standard concepts are stored as 0 (not NULL), with the original value preserved in *_source_value (and optionally *_source_concept_id when a source concept exists).
    3. Failures are observable — Reject tables and QC tables record date/numeric parse failures, missing joins, mapping coverage, and referential integrity so issues can be inspected and fixed.

    Workflow: Execution Order

    Scripts live under inst/sql/ in numbered folders. The runner executes all .sql files in lexicographic order (by folder, then file name). The following is the full sequence and intent of each phase.

    Phase 0 — Admin and setup

    File Purpose
    00_admin/00_create_stg_schema.sql Create schema stg if not exists.
    00_admin/01_create_etl_log_tables.sql Create stg.etl_run_log (run_id, started_at, ended_at, status, git_sha) and stg.etl_step_log (run_id, step_name, file_name, started_at, ended_at, status, rows_affected, notes).
    00_admin/02_clear_stg_tables.sql Drop all stg.* tables (reject, maps, staged, QC) so the ETL can be re-run. Does not drop cdm.* or src.*.

    Phase 1 — Staging (typing and normalization)

    Source columns are varchar. Each staging script parses dates (with COALESCE(try_strptime(..., fmt1), try_strptime(..., fmt2), ...)), casts numerics with try_cast, and trims/normalizes strings. Rows that fail parsing (e.g. non-null date string but no parseable date) are written to a corresponding reject table.

    File Staging table Reject table Notes
    10_stage/10_stage_enrollment.sql stg.enrollment stg.reject_enrollment_dates DOB parsed; gender/race/address trimmed and lowercased.
    11_stage_provider.sql stg.provider stg.reject_provider DOB, sex, specialty cleaned.
    12_stage_encounter.sql stg.encounter stg.reject_encounter Encounter_DateTime → encounter_date/datetime; appt_type, clinic_type, SOAP_Note.
    13_stage_death.sql stg.death stg.reject_death DOD → death_date, death_datetime.
    14_stage_problem.sql stg.problem stg.reject_problem Onset_Date, Resolution_Date; Problem_Code/Type normalized.
    15_stage_med_orders.sql stg.medication_orders stg.reject_med_orders Order_Date, Last_Filled_Date; Dose, Qty_Ordered, Refills via try_cast; Drug_NDC normalized (strip dashes).
    16_stage_med_fulfillment.sql stg.medication_fulfillment stg.reject_med_fulfillment Dispense_Date; Dispense_Qty, Days_Of_Supply, Fill_No.
    17_stage_current_meds.sql stg.current_medications stg.reject_current_meds Last_Filled_Date; Refills, Days_Of_Supply.
    18_stage_immunization.sql stg.immunization stg.reject_immunization Vaccination_Date; Dose; Vaccine_CVX trimmed.
    19_stage_lab_orders.sql stg.lab_orders stg.reject_lab_orders Order_Date; Test_LOINC normalized.
    20_stage_lab_results.sql stg.lab_results stg.reject_lab_results Date_Collected, Date_Resulted; Numeric_Result; LOINC/Units.
    21_stage_vital_sign.sql stg.vital_sign stg.reject_vital_sign Encounter_Date; Height, Weight, SBP, DBP, Pulse, Respiration, Temperature.
    22_stage_allergy.sql stg.allergy stg.reject_allergy Onset_Date; Drug_Code, Allergen, etc. normalized.
    23_stage_therapy_orders.sql stg.therapy_orders — Code, Vocabulary; no dates in source.
    24_stage_therapy_actions.sql stg.therapy_actions — Code, Vocabulary, Result, Target_Area.

    Phase 2 — Keys (deterministic integer ID maps)

    Maps assign stable integer IDs to source keys using ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY <stable_key>). No randomness; same source data yields same IDs across runs.

    File Map table Source of keys
    20_keys/30_map_person.sql stg.map_person(member_id, person_id) Distinct member_id from union of all tables that contain Member_ID (enrollment, encounter, death, problem, medication_orders, immunization, lab_results, vital_sign, allergy, therapy_orders, therapy_actions, current_medications). Ensures persons who appear only outside enrollment still get an ID.
    31_map_provider.sql stg.map_provider(provider_id_source, provider_id) Distinct Provider_ID from provider, encounter, medication_orders, immunization, lab_results, problem.
    32_map_visit.sql stg.map_visit(encounter_id_source, visit_occurrence_id) Distinct Encounter_ID from stg.encounter.
    33_map_location.sql stg.map_location(location_key, location_id) Distinct concatenation of address_1, address_2, city, state, zip from stg.enrollment.
    34_map_care_site.sql stg.map_care_site(care_site_key, care_site_id) Distinct Clinic_ID from stg.encounter (optional).

    Phase 3 — Vocabulary and concept mapping

    Mapping tables/views resolve source values to OMOP concept IDs. Unmapped → 0; source values are always preserved in the CDM *_source_value fields.

    File Map table Strategy
    30_vocab/40_map_gender.sql stg.map_gender Lookup: female/f→8532, male/m→8507, unknown/other→8551; enrollment-specific values inserted with 0.
    41_map_race.sql stg.map_race Lookup: white, black, asian, etc. to standard race concept_ids; rest → 0.
    42_map_visit_concept.sql stg.map_visit_concept (appt_type, clinic_type) → visit_concept_id; default 44813942 (Outpatient).
    43_map_drug.sql stg.map_drug_order NDC → cdm.concept (vocabulary_id=‘NDC’) → concept_relationship ‘Maps to’ → standard RxNorm; drug_name-only rows added with concept_id 0.
    44_map_loinc_measurement.sql stg.map_loinc_measurement Test_LOINC → cdm.concept (vocabulary_id=‘LOINC’); unmapped → 0.
    45_map_units.sql stg.map_units Unit strings (mg, cm, kg, etc.) → unit_concept_id; distinct units from staging added as 0 if not in lookup.
    46_map_condition.sql stg.map_condition Problem_Code → concept (ICD10/ICD9/SNOMED/Medcin) → Maps to standard condition; unmapped → 0.
    47_map_vitals.sql stg.map_vitals Hardcoded: vital name (Height, Weight, SystolicBP, …) → measurement_concept_id and default unit (LOINC/OMOP placeholders).
    48_map_allergy.sql stg.map_allergy v1: observation_concept_id = 0; source_value = Allergen or Drug_Code.
    49_map_therapy.sql stg.map_therapy Code + Vocabulary → concept (CPT4, HCPCS, SNOMED, ICD10PCS) → Maps to standard procedure; else 0.

    Phase 4 — CDM core tables

    Load order respects foreign keys: location and care_site first, then provider, person, visit, note, death.

    File CDM table Source and decisions
    40_cdm_core/50_load_location.sql cdm.location From stg.map_location; address fields from first matching enrollment row per location_key. Insert only if location_id not already present.
    51_load_care_site.sql cdm.care_site From stg.map_care_site; optional.
    52_load_provider.sql cdm.provider From map_provider + stg.provider; specialty_concept_id null in v1.
    53_load_person.sql cdm.person From map_person + stg.enrollment. Only persons with parseable DOB in enrollment are inserted (year_of_birth NOT NULL). Others logged in stg.reject_person_missing_dob. Demographics from enrollment; location_id from map_location.
    54_load_visit_occurrence.sql cdm.visit_occurrence From stg.encounter + map_visit, map_person, map_visit_concept, map_provider, map_care_site. visit_type_concept_id = 44818517 (EHR). Visit end date = start when no end time. Encounters with member_id not in map_person → stg.reject_visit_missing_person.
    55_load_note.sql cdm.note SOAP_Note from encounter; note_id = 900000000 + row_number (deterministic). note_type/class/encoding/language from config. Reject table for notes missing person/visit.
    56_load_death.sql cdm.death From stg.death + map_person. death_type_concept_id from config. Reject rows with no matching person → stg.reject_death_load.

    Phase 5 — CDM clinical tables

    File CDM table Source and decisions
    50_cdm_clinical/60_load_condition_occurrence.sql cdm.condition_occurrence stg.problem; condition_occurrence_id deterministic; condition_start_date = onset_date, fallback to encounter_date when onset missing; condition_concept_id from map_condition (0 if unmapped). Reject: missing person.
    61_load_drug_exposure_orders.sql cdm.drug_exposure stg.medication_orders; drug_type 38000177 (EHR order). drug_exposure_id in 700M range. End date = order_date when no days_supply.
    62_load_drug_exposure_fulfillment.sql cdm.drug_exposure stg.medication_fulfillment joined to medication_orders for member_id and drug; drug_type 38000230 (dispensed). End date = dispense_date + days_supply - 1 when present. Rows with Order_ID not in orders → stg.reject_fulfillment_no_order.
    63_load_drug_exposure_immunization.sql cdm.drug_exposure stg.immunization; drug_type 38000280 (immunization). drug_concept_id 0 in v1 (CVX mapping optional).
    64_load_measurement_vitals.sql cdm.measurement stg.vital_sign unpivoted (one row per non-null Height, Weight, SBP, DBP, Pulse, Respiration, Temperature). measurement_concept_id from map_vitals; unit from map_units or map_vitals default.
    65_load_measurement_labs.sql cdm.measurement stg.lab_results; measurement_concept_id from map_loinc_measurement (0 if unmapped). measurement_date = date_resulted else date_collected. Reject: missing person or measurement_date.
    66_load_observation_allergy.sql cdm.observation stg.allergy; observation_concept_id 0 in v1; observation_type 32859. observation_source_value = Allergen or Drug_Code. Reject when onset_date missing or person missing.
    67_load_procedure_therapy.sql cdm.procedure_occurrence stg.therapy_orders + therapy_actions; procedure_date from encounter via Encounter_ID. procedure_concept_id from map_therapy (0 if unmapped). Reject: missing person or missing encounter_date.

    Phase 6 — Derived CDM

    File CDM table Logic
    60_cdm_derived/80_load_observation_period.sql cdm.observation_period Per person: min/max of all event dates from visit_occurrence, condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, measurement, observation, procedure_occurrence, death. observation_period_id in 900M range. period_type_concept_id 32821. Insert only for persons not already having an observation_period.

    Phase 7 — QC and observability

    File QC table Content
    90_qc/90_row_counts.sql stg.qc_row_counts Row counts for key stg.* and cdm.* tables (run_id from etl_run_log).
    91_mapping_coverage.sql stg.qc_mapping_coverage Per clinical table: total rows, unmapped count, unmapped % (*_concept_id = 0).
    92_referential_integrity.sql stg.qc_orphans Orphan checks: visit_occurrence.person_id not in person; condition/drug/measurement person_id not in person; visit_occurrence_id not in visit_occurrence when not null.
    93_parse_failures.sql stg.qc_parse_failures Count of rows in each stg.reject_* table.
    94_required_fields.sql stg.qc_required_fields Violations of NOT NULL (person.year_of_birth, visit dates, condition_start_date, etc.). Runner can be extended to fail if any violation_count > 0.

    Key Design Decisions

    Deterministic integer IDs

    • person_id, provider_id, visit_occurrence_id, location_id, care_site_id come from stg.map_* tables built with ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY <stable_source_key>). Same source data ⇒ same IDs on every run.
    • Clinical table IDs (condition_occurrence_id, drug_exposure_id, measurement_id, observation_id, procedure_occurrence_id) use fixed numeric ranges (e.g. 800M, 700M, 500M, 400M, 300M, 200M, 100M) plus row_number so they are deterministic and non-overlapping.

    Unmapped concepts → 0

    • OMOP requires concept_id columns to be NOT NULL where applicable; “no mapping” is represented as 0 (config: concept_id_unknown), not NULL.
    • Original values are always stored in *_source_value (and *_source_concept_id when a source concept exists but no standard mapping).

    Person and DOB

    • year_of_birth in cdm.person is NOT NULL. The ETL only inserts persons who have a parseable DOB in stg.enrollment. Members who appear only in other tables or have null/unparseable DOB are not inserted into person and are logged in stg.reject_person_missing_dob. No sentinel year or inference from encounter.Patient_DOB in v1.

    Medication orders vs. fulfillment

    • Both medication_orders and medication_fulfillment are loaded into cdm.drug_exposure with different drug_type_concept_id (38000177 order, 38000230 dispensed).
    • Fulfillment rows are joined to medication_orders on Order_ID to get member_id and drug fields; if Order_ID is missing in orders, the row is logged in stg.reject_fulfillment_no_order and not loaded.
    • Config flag prefer_fulfillment is documented; if set true, the ETL could exclude order rows that have a matching fulfillment (logic can be added in 61/62).

    Visit and encounter

    • Encounter_ID is the backbone for visit linking. visit_occurrence_id = map_visit(encounter_id). When Encounter_ID is missing on a clinical row, visit_occurrence_id is left NULL but person_id and date are still loaded.
    • Visit end date: When the source has no end time, visit_end_date = visit_start_date (same-day encounter).

    Reject tables

    • Every staging script that parses dates or numerics writes failing rows to a reject table (e.g. non-null DOB that did not parse → stg.reject_enrollment_dates). Load scripts add rejects for missing person (e.g. reject_visit_missing_person, reject_condition_missing_person). QC script 93 summarizes counts per reject table.

    Allergy and therapy

    • Allergy: v1 uses observation_concept_id = 0; observation_source_value holds Allergen or Drug_Code. Optional future: map Drug_Code/Drug_Vocab to drug concepts.
    • Therapy: procedure_date is taken from encounter via Encounter_ID. Rows with no encounter or no encounter_date are rejected.

    Configuration

    Configuration is read from inst/extdata/config.yml (or a path you pass). Key sections:

    • schemas: src, stg, cdm — used for schema substitution in SQL (@cdmDatabaseSchema, {cdm}, {stg}, {src}).
    • concept_id_unknown: 0.
    • date_formats: list of strptime formats (used conceptually; SQL scripts use explicit try_strptime sequences).
    • type_concept_ids: visit_type (44818517), default_visit (44813942), condition (32818), drug orders (38000177), dispensed (38000230), immunization (38000280), measurement vitals (32817), labs (32827), observation allergy (32859), note type/class (44813942, 44814639), language (4180186), encoding (44815386), procedure (38000268), period (32821), death (32817).
    • prefer_fulfillment: when true (future), exclude medication_orders that have a matching fulfillment.

    You can override config by passing a list to run_etl(con, config = my_list) or a path with run_etl(con, config_path = "path/to/config.yml").

    Running the ETL

    Full run 

    library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(ETLDelphi)

con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), "path/to/your.duckdb")
run_etl(con)
dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)

    Dry run (list scripts only) 

    run_etl(con, dry_run = TRUE)

    From / to step

    Run only a subset of the pipeline (e.g. from CDM core through clinical, or only QC):

    # From admin through staging
run_etl(con, from_step = "00_admin", to_step = "02_clear")

# From CDM core through end (assumes stg already populated)
run_etl(con, from_step = "40_cdm_core")

# Only QC
run_etl(con, from_step = "90_qc", to_step = "94_required_fields")

    Custom config or SQL directory 

    run_etl(con, config_path = "/path/to/config.yml")
run_etl(con, sql_dir = "/path/to/sql")

    QC and Observability

    After a run, inspect:

    • stg.qc_row_counts — row counts per table.
    • stg.qc_mapping_coverage — unmapped counts and percentages per clinical table.
    • stg.qc_orphans — referential integrity (orphan person_id, visit_occurrence_id).
    • stg.qc_parse_failures — counts per reject table.
    • stg.qc_required_fields — NOT NULL violations.

    Reject tables (e.g. stg.reject_enrollment_dates, stg.reject_visit_missing_person) hold the actual failing rows for review. The runner can be extended to abort if stg.qc_required_fields has any violation_count > 0.

    Updating Mappings and Limitations

    • Gender/race: Edit 40_map_gender.sql and 41_map_race.sql; add (source_value, concept_id) or rely on the INSERT that adds enrollment values with 0.
    • Visit concept: Adjust rules in 42_map_visit_concept.sql (appt_type, clinic_type → visit_concept_id).
    • Drug: NDC mapping uses cdm.concept and cdm.concept_relationship; extend for drug name if needed.
    • Condition: 46_map_condition.sql uses Problem_Code and vocabulary (ICD10, ICD9, SNOMED, Medcin).
    • Units: Add unit strings in 45_map_units.sql.
    • Vitals: 47_map_vitals.sql uses placeholder concept IDs; replace with your vocabulary’s measurement and unit concepts.

    Known limitations: Person load requires DOB in enrollment; SOAP_Note may be truncated (varchar(255) in source); allergy observation concept is 0 in v1; observation_period is derived from event dates (no death truncation rule applied in v1). The prefer_fulfillment flag is not yet implemented in the load scripts.

    Summary

    The ETL is a SQL-first, ordered pipeline with a clear src → stg → cdm flow, deterministic integer IDs, explicit concept mapping (unmapped = 0), and reject + QC tables for observability. The R package orchestrates execution and logging; all business logic lives in the SQL scripts under inst/sql/, so changes to rules or mappings are made there and documented in this vignette.

    Developed by Adam Black.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
